news.title: Shriram Properties targets ₹3,750-4,000 crore pre-sales in FY27, expects Pune to overtake Chennai
news.para: Shriram Properties expects to clock ₹3,750-4,000 crore of pre-sales in FY27 and remains on track to meet its FY28 targets, said Murali Malayappan, chairman & managing director, and Gopalakrishnan J, executive director & CEO, Shriram Properties. The company plans to deepen its presence in Bengaluru, Chennai, Pune and Kolkata, while evaluating Mumbai and selectively looking at office, data centre and rental housing opportunities.

news.title: PPCB flags compliance gaps, urges builders to follow EC norms in Zirakpur
news.para: The Punjab Pollution Control Board (PPCB) met with the Builders Welfare Association in Zirakpur to emphasize environmental compliance and adherence to Environmental Clearance (EC) conditions. Officials urged builders to display guidelines on-site, accurately self-file applications, and prepare for monsoon by cleaning rainwater harvesting systems and ensuring efficient STPs for sustainable urban growth.

news.title: Hyderabad's Raidurg area witnesses a record-breaking land auction as one acre fetches ₹237 crore
news.para: Hyderabad's Raidurg witnessed a record-breaking land auction as one acre fetched Rs 237 crore, significantly surpassing previous benchmarks. Gowra Ventures Pvt Ltd secured the 6.29-acre parcel for IT and mixed development. This phenomenal price, 70% over the reserve, underscores investor confidence in Telangana's burgeoning economic landscape and Hyderabad's prime real estate appeal.

news.title: Godrej Family Office, JSW Family Office, Lodha and Sattva Family Office invest in SILA
news.para: SILA has added Godrej Family Office, JSW Family Office, Lodha and Sattva Family Office as investors after Permira’s recent $100 million investment in the company. The business services platform operates across more than 125 cities, manages over 450 million sq ft of real estate and employs over 60,000 people.

news.title: Top 11 listed developers’ pre-sales rise 18% to ₹1.48 lakh crore in FY26: Anarock
news.para: The combined pre-sales of 11 listed real estate developers increased 18% year-on-year to ₹1.48 lakh crore in FY26, according to Anarock. Developers with higher exposure to premium and luxury housing, along with those expanding beyond their home markets, recorded stronger growth during the year.

news.title: Online plan approval system falters, Goa's town planning department still leans on offline route
news.para: Goa's town planning department has processed over 6,000 technical clearances but only a handful of construction licenses online since 2020. The Building Plan Approval Management System, intended for transparency, sees limited use. Data reveals delays in approvals, with offline methods seemingly preferred. While the system now covers more projects, experts suggest wider adoption for better business ease.

news.title: Mumbai developer accuses former partner of cheating him out of ₹11.51 crore
news.para: A Mumbai developer has accused his former partner of cheating him out of Rs 11.51 crore by allegedly concealing the prior sale of 19 flats in a Bandra East slum rehabilitation project. The Economic Offences Wing has registered an FIR for cheating, forgery, and breach of trust against the accused, who is alleged to have diverted sale proceeds.

news.title: Punjab RERA orders ₹25 lakh payout for delayed possession, sets key precedent
news.para: Punjab RERA ruled that project timelines for homebuyers must be counted from the initial booking date, not the sale agreement execution. This decision mandates a real estate promoter to pay approximately Rs 25 lakh in delay interest to PEC faculty members, reinforcing homebuyers' rights from the date of booking and payment.

news.title: Karnataka RERA recovery cell plan shelved, welfare bodies disappointed
news.para: A dedicated cell to recover dues for homebuyers from Rera adjudicated cases has been dropped. Instead, a special deputy commissioner for Bengaluru Urban district has been appointed to speed up recovery. This move aims to address the slow recovery of amounts owed to homebuyers. Homebuyers' associations are calling for better coordination and time-bound disposal of cases.

news.title: MahaRERA pulls up Mumbai builder again over defects, delayed conveyance
news.para: MahaRERA has ordered Vasant Oasis project promoters to fix defects like leakage and structural issues in common areas. The regulator also mandated conveyance to the housing society and formation of an apex body, rejecting arguments for indefinite delay. While layout changes were not proven, promoters must fulfill statutory obligations.

news.title: Unitech's net loss widens to ₹441 crore in Q4 FY26
news.para: Unitech Ltd reported a consolidated net loss of Rs 441 crore for the quarter ended March 2026, an increase from the previous year. Total income saw a rise in the fourth quarter and for the full fiscal year. The company continues to face a significant cash crunch, leading to project delays and impacting thousands of customers.

news.title: LIC looks to improve return from real estates, to explore option of separate subsidiary
news.para: LIC is actively reviewing its vast real estate portfolio, estimated at over Rs 60,000 crore, to improve returns for policyholders and shareholders. The insurer is exploring options like a separate subsidiary for greater efficiency in managing these assets. Additionally, LIC is prepared for further stake dilution by the government, awaiting the right market conditions for subsequent public offerings.

news.title: Creditors realised ₹4 lakh crore; cases involving ₹14 lakh crore settled in pre-admission stage: IBC
news.para: India's insolvency law has facilitated over Rs 4 lakh crore in creditor realisations from resolved cases. Additionally, more than 30,000 pre-admission stage cases, involving nearly Rs 14 lakh crore, were settled. This code has significantly altered debtor-creditor dynamics, promoting timely resolution of financial stress.

news.title: SC seeks responses of Centre, ED, RBI on plea alleging siphoning of homebuyers' funds
news.para: The Supreme Court has issued notices to the Centre, ED, RBI, and several real estate firms regarding allegations of siphoning off thousands of crores collected from homebuyers in Noida and Yamuna Expressway projects. The plea highlights a systemic issue of developers diverting funds, leading to project insolvency and buyers being left stranded.

news.title: Fire brigade cracks down on housing societies in Pune over safety lapses
news.para: A recent fire in a Pune housing society exposed widespread fire safety negligence across Maharashtra. Inspections revealed major lapses, including obstructed access and a lack of basic firefighting equipment. Despite strengthened laws and numerous licensed agencies, many societies remain unaware of or neglect their mandatory compliance responsibilities.

news.title: Pune police register FIR against Runwal Enterprises' MD in Viman Nagar land dispute case
news.para: Pune Police have registered a case against Runwal Enterprises MD Subodh Runwal and six others following a court order concerning a land dispute in Viman Nagar. The FIR alleges forged documents and illegal transfer of rights over a 3.93-hectare land parcel, stemming from a joint venture agreement.

news.title: No hand raised to buy Ludhiana’s ₹197-crore ghost complex
news.para: A 77-unit derelict property in Ludhiana failed to attract any bidders at auction, marking an 18-year streak of failure for the Ludhiana Improvement Trust. The state-backed developer is now reconsidering the reserve price for the 2.22-acre property after regional officials ignored internal warnings about its inflated valuation, which was expected to fetch ₹197 crore.

news.title: Asia Pacific real estate investment rises 19% in Q1 2026: Report
news.para: Real estate investment in Asia Pacific rose 19.2% year-on-year in Q1 2026, led by office assets and renewed cross-border capital flows, according to Savills. India remained among the markets drawing investor interest in industrial, logistics and data centre-linked assets.

news.title: Delhi draft B&B policy proposes eight room, 16 bed limit; CCTV mandatory
news.para: Delhi is set to introduce a draft policy for Bed and Breakfast units, categorizing them into Silver and Gold. The proposal mandates CCTV cameras, police verification, and adherence to safety and hygiene standards for residential properties offering accommodation. This aims to enhance tourism infrastructure and provide income opportunities for residents.

news.title: Visible road dust, construction waste found during inspection drive in Ghaziabad
news.para: An inspection by the Commission for Air Quality Management (CAQM) in Ghaziabad revealed 24 instances of high visible road dust and construction waste. The commission has directed authorities to intensify dust mitigation measures, including mechanized sweeping and water sprinkling, to address the issue.
